title: clinopodium alpinum subsp. sardoum
intro: Clinopodium alpinum subsp. sardoum, also known as Sardinian mountain calamintha, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to Sardinia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and carminative effects, which are attributed to its essential oils and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Clinopodium alpinum subsp. sardoum include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, such as bloating and cramps, as well as its potential to reduce stress and anxiety. This plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used in traditional medicine to promote relaxation, soothe digestive problems, and support overall well-being.
